They do not contradict each other. Both can be true at the same time.

Let's check "refines": "Adds detail. Does NOT contradict. Both remain true. e.g. old 'we dropped Docker' + new 'we dropped Docker because of build times'". The new claim doesn't add detail to the project name, it adds a new fact about the project. So "unrelated" is the best fit, as they are about the same topic (the Meridian project) but both can be true at once.

Wait, is it "unrelated"?
"Same topic, but both can be true at once. Leave the old claim alone."
Yes, this fits perfectly.
